author: @Karabetter
text: Suggestion:  555 timer ICs were a super little building block.  However, the original designs were bi-polar and consumed quite a bit of power on their own.  Since that time, CMOS versions of the 555 became available.  They have all the same pinout, voltage and driving specs, but use MUCH less power. Price difference was insignificant. Please select those for your project. :)

author: @uksuperrascal
text: To Luke is this your first kit ? - In the 70's you could buy conductive paste - I used it to repair car rear window demisters, but you had to wait a day for the paste to dry before it would conduct, how long do you have to wait before your ink will conduct?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text:
It depends on how thick you apply the ink, Leaving it to air dry i would say around 1-2 hours maybe 3. But if you whack it with a hair dryer in-between coats it takes about 5-10 minutes on a low setting.
It will be slightly conductive while its abit wet, but i would recommend waiting a little bit til its dry hahah.  
Hoped this helped!!
